title: Finding Status v1
description:
Authoritative finding status — the output of
lib.finding_events.OutcomeStore.derive_finding_status(). Per ADR-0002,
this is the SOLE owner of the reducer; lib/h1report.py and
bin/lab-h1-report status delegate to it and only layer H1-specific
presentation on top.

Precedence (roadmap section 7.4):
  1. outcomes.jsonl (platform ground truth — this is what this reducer
     consumes in the Phase 1 MVP)
  2. record.json (human submission receipt — for submission_state)
  3. manifest.json (prepared package)
  4. audit events
  5. bounty_log.md (editable, lowest authority)

Phase 1 MVP scope: outcomes.jsonl + record.json only. The remaining
layers are folded in by later phases. Until then, technical_verdict,
impact_demonstrated, and confidence carry conservative defaults
(inconclusive / false / 0.0) that will be replaced by the event-ledger
reducer in Phase 2.

workspace_id.description:
UUID of the workspace from <workspace>/.lab/workspace.json, if that
file exists. null when the workspace has no workspace.json (Phase 1
MVP does not require it).

report_id.description:
HackerOne report ID this status pertains to. Matches the report_id in
the outcome events and in the workspace's record.json.

technical_verdict.description:
Agent's technical verdict on whether the vulnerability is real.
SI-031: the reducer now folds this from the workspace event ledger
(<workspace>/.lab/events.jsonl). The latest non-null technical_verdict
across all events for this report_id wins (a later event that revises
the verdict overrides an earlier one). When no event carries the
field, the conservative default 'inconclusive' is returned.

reportability.description:
Recommendation on whether to submit (or re-submit) to HackerOne.
Derived from platform_state in Phase 1: duplicate or informative =>
do_not_report; otherwise gather_more_evidence (conservative).

platform_state.description:
Latest outcome state observed for this report_id, or null when no
outcome has been recorded yet. One of the outcome-v1 state enum values.

platform_state_at.description:
occurred_at of the latest outcome event, or null when no outcome has
been recorded yet.

impact_demonstrated.description:
Whether the finding demonstrated actual impact (not just a theoretical
or empty response). SI-031: the reducer now reads the report's
validated `poc.state_changed` frontmatter field (validated by `check`
as a bool) so it is evidence, not self-asserted prose. When the field
is absent or the report is unparseable, returns false (conservative).

confidence.description:
Confidence in the technical verdict, 0.0 to 1.0. SI-031: the reducer
now folds this from the workspace event ledger. The latest non-null
confidence across all events for this report_id wins.

submission_state.description:
State of the local submission receipt.
'not_submitted' = no record.json found.
'submitted' = record.json exists (kept for backward compat; prefer
'recorded' for new code).
'recorded' = record.json exists with a valid schema and report_id.

last_event_ts.description:
ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of the last event that informed this status.
In Phase 1, this is the occurred_at of the latest outcome event, or
the submitted_at from record.json if no outcome exists, or the current
time if neither exists.

duplicate_of.description:
Populated when platform_state='duplicate' — the H1 report ID of the
original. Mirrored from the latest outcome event for convenience.

duplicate_original_state.description:
Populated when platform_state='duplicate' — the state of the original
report. Mirrored from the latest outcome event.
